question_text: Name one difference between transverse waves and longitudinal waves.
correct_answer: Transverse waves vibrate perpendicular (at right angles) to the direction of wave travel, while longitudinal waves vibrate parallel (in the same direction) as the wave travels. (Accept: Transverse waves have peaks and troughs; longitudinal waves have compressions and rarefactions.)
explanation: In transverse waves, particles move up and down while the wave moves left and right. In longitudinal waves, particles move back and forth in the same direction the wave is going.
